title: pyrus torminalis var. glaberrima
intro: Pyrus torminalis var. glaberrima, also known as the bitter pear or wild pear, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its bitter and astringent properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of torminalin, a glycoside with potential medicinal effects. The medicinal properties of Pyrus torminalis var. glaberrima are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and respiratory infections.
